title: Immigrant Justice Corps 2026 Community Fellowship: Fully Funded 3-Year Opportunity for Aspiring Immigration Advocates
summary: The 2026 Community Fellowship by Immigrant Justice Corps (IJC) stands as one of the most impactful and prestigious opportunities for young professionals passionate about immigration justice, social equity, and legal advocacy in the United States. As the nation’s first and only fellowship program dedicated exclusively to immigration, IJC continues to bridge the justice gap by placing talented graduates into leading legal service organizations. With applications now open for its twelfth cohort, this fully funded, three-year fellowship offers a transformative career pathway for individuals committed to supporting low-income immigrant communities while gaining hands-on legal experience. What is the IJC Community Fellowship? The IJC Community Fellowship is a structured, paid program designed to recruit recent college graduates and immerse them in frontline immigration advocacy. Fellows are placed within highly respected legal service organizations, where they directly support underserved immigrant populations. Currently, IJC hosts over 130 fellows across more than 30 U.S. states, contributing significantly to expanding access to justice. The 2026 cohort introduces a unique opportunity with three-year placements , allowing fellows to develop expertise, build professional networks, and achieve formal accreditation in immigration legal practice. Fellowship Structure and Professional Development The Community Fellowship is carefully designed to provide both practical experience and professional certification. Accreditation Pathway Fellows are expected to progress through the U.S. Department of Justice Recognition Accreditation Program: Partial Accreditation : Achieved within the first year (ideally within 6–12 months), enabling fellows to represent clients before U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services. Full Accreditation : Targeted between 12–18 months, allowing fellows to practice before Immigration Courts. This structured pathway ensures that fellows graduate with strong legal competencies and recognized credentials. Key Responsibilities of Community Fellows Participants in the program take on meaningful and impactful roles, including: Managing individual caseloads under legal supervision Providing direct legal services and representation to immigrants Translating complex immigration policies into accessible language Supporting communities through culturally competent advocacy Engaging with diverse populations facing legal and systemic barriers This immersive experience equips fellows with both technical legal skills and deep community engagement expertise. Fellowship Benefits The IJC Community Fellowship offers a comprehensive benefits package, making it one of the most competitive programs in the field: Full-time salary Employee benefits Extensive training and mentorship Hands-on legal experience Professional accreditation pathway Long-term career development in immigration law and advocacy Placement Locations For the 2026 cohort, placements are expected to be based in: New York City Long Island Upstate New York Fellows must be willing to relocate to their assigned location. While some placements may offer hybrid work arrangements, fully remote participation is not available. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be current undergraduate students graduating by Spring 2026 or recent graduates (no earlier than Spring 2024) Hold an Associate or Bachelor’s degree Be eligible for work authorization in the United States for the full duration of the fellowship Demonstrate proficiency in English and at least one additional language Spanish (including indigenous dialects) is strongly preferred Applicants with DACA or TPS status are encouraged to apply. Application Components Candidates must submit a complete application package including: Completed application form Resume or CV (maximum two pages) Unofficial academic transcripts Statement of Interest (maximum 500 words) Essay on immigration policy (maximum 500 words) At least one letter of recommendation (two recommended) The written components are critical and should reflect the applicant’s passion, cultural awareness, and commitment to immigration justice. Selection Process The selection process is competitive and takes place in multiple stages: Application Review (Early May 2026) Interviews (Mid-May 2026, virtual format) Final Matching Process (Late May 2026) Fellowship Agreements Issued (June 2026) Successful applicants are matched with host organizations based on mutual preferences and program needs. Application Deadline All application materials, including recommendation letters, must be submitted by: Sunday, May 3, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. (ET) Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin early and maintain close communication with their referees to ensure timely submission. Why Apply for the IJC Community Fellowship? This fellowship is more than a professional opportunity—it is a platform to create meaningful social impact. Fellows become part of a national movement working to ensure equitable access to justice for immigrant communities. By combining legal training, community engagement, and professional mentorship, the IJC Community Fellowship prepares participants for long-term careers in law, policy, and advocacy. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Immigrant Justice Corps 2026 Community Fellowship: Fully Funded 3-Year Opportunity for Aspiring Immigration Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Paid Remote Climate & Conservation Finance Internship 2026 with The Nature Conservancy
summary: Are you looking to gain hands-on experience in climate finance, policy, and global sustainability? The Climate Conservation Finance Policy Internship 2026 with The Nature Conservancy (TNC) is a paid, fully remote opportunity designed for students and recent graduates eager to work at the intersection of finance, policy, and environmental impact. Over eight weeks, interns will contribute to real-world projects, support global initiatives, and build valuable professional skills. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged. About the Opportunity The Nature Conservancy (TNC) is one of the world’s leading environmental organizations, working to protect lands and waters globally. Through its Internships @TNC program , the organization offers structured, paid opportunities for emerging professionals to apply their academic knowledge to real-world conservation challenges. This specific internship is part of the Climate and Conservation Finance Policy (CCFP) team , which focuses on financing climate and nature solutions in emerging markets and developing economies. The team works with governments, financial institutions, and global stakeholders to design and implement innovative financial mechanisms that support sustainability and economic resilience. Interns in this role will contribute to projects that explore tools such as debt-for-nature swaps, green finance frameworks, ecosystem service payments, and climate-related financial strategies . They will also support research and policy development aimed at integrating nature and biodiversity into economic systems and financial decision-making. The internship runs for 8 weeks, from 15 June to 7 August 2026 , and is conducted remotely. Interns will participate in onboarding, training, mentorship, and networking activities, while also completing a capstone project that showcases their learning and contributions. Why This Opportunity Matters Climate change and environmental degradation are among the most pressing global challenges today. Addressing these issues requires innovative financial solutions that can support sustainable development while protecting natural ecosystems. The work of the CCFP team at The Nature Conservancy is critical in advancing these solutions by helping governments and institutions design financial systems that integrate environmental priorities. This internship allows participants to contribute to efforts that have real-world policy and economic implications , particularly in developing regions where the need for sustainable solutions is urgent. By joining this program, interns gain insight into how global environmental strategies are developed and implemented, while also building the skills needed to contribute to long-term sustainability goals. What You’ll Gain This internship offers a strong foundation for careers in sustainability, policy, and international development: Paid Experience: Earn $22 per hour while gaining professional experience in a global organization. Real-World Impact: Work on projects that influence climate and conservation finance strategies worldwide. Skill Development: Build expertise in research, policy writing, data analysis, and stakeholder engagement. Mentorship and Training: Receive guidance from experienced professionals and participate in structured learning sessions. Global Exposure: Contribute to international initiatives and support major events such as climate and biodiversity forums. This opportunity is particularly valuable for individuals aiming to enter careers in climate policy, development finance, or environmental economics . Eligibility To be eligible for this internship, applicants must: Be currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate program , or be a recent graduate (within six months) Have a background in policy, finance, economics, environmental studies, or related fields Demonstrate experience in research, writing, and communication Be fluent in English Preferred qualifications include: Experience in climate finance, international development, or public policy Familiarity with data analysis or visualization tools Ability to work independently in a remote and collaborative environment Knowledge of additional languages such as Spanish, Portuguese, or French Application Process and Deadline To apply for the Climate Conservation Finance Policy Internship: Prepare Your Application: Update your CV and ensure your experience aligns with the role requirements. Apply Online: Submit your application through The Nature Conservancy’s official careers portal. Rolling Review: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and the position may close once a strong candidate pool is identified. Internship Duration: 15 June – 7 August 2026 Location: Remote Compensation: $22/hour To learn more and apply, visit the official Nature Conservancy internship job posting . title: Susan Carroll Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Investigative Journalism Opportunity for Emerging Reporters
summary: The Susan Carroll Fellowship, offered by the Investigative Reporters and Editors (IRE), is a prestigious and highly competitive opportunity designed to empower emerging journalists with the skills, mentorship, and financial support needed to excel in investigative reporting. Named in honor of the late investigative journalist Susan Carroll, this fellowship aims to continue her legacy of impactful, data-driven journalism that holds institutions accountable and amplifies underreported stories. This fellowship is particularly significant in today’s media landscape, where investigative journalism plays a crucial role in uncovering truths, promoting transparency, and driving societal change. The program is structured to support early-career journalists by providing them with access to training, resources, and a network of experienced professionals in the field. Overview of the Susan Carroll Fellowship The Susan Carroll Fellowship is designed for journalists who are passionate about investigative reporting but may lack access to the resources and training needed to fully develop their potential. Through this fellowship, selected participants receive financial assistance to attend IRE’s renowned training programs, where they can gain hands-on experience and learn advanced investigative techniques. The fellowship places a strong emphasis on data journalism, a field that Susan Carroll was deeply committed to. Participants are trained to analyze complex datasets, uncover hidden patterns, and transform raw information into compelling stories that inform and engage the public. What the Fellowship Offers One of the most attractive aspects of the Susan Carroll Fellowship is its comprehensive support package. Fellows receive funding that covers the cost of attending IRE training events, including registration fees, travel expenses, and accommodation. This ensures that financial constraints do not hinder talented journalists from accessing world-class training. In addition to financial support, fellows gain access to: Intensive investigative journalism training sessions Workshops on data analysis, reporting techniques, and storytelling Networking opportunities with leading journalists and media professionals Mentorship from experienced investigative reporters Exposure to cutting-edge tools and technologies used in modern journalism These benefits collectively create a transformative learning experience that equips fellows with the skills needed to produce impactful investigative stories. Training and Learning Experience The fellowship provides an immersive learning environment where participants engage in practical, hands-on training. Workshops are led by industry experts who share real-world insights and best practices. Topics typically include: Data collection and cleaning Statistical analysis and visualization Freedom of information requests Ethical considerations in investigative journalism Digital security for journalists By the end of the program, fellows are expected to have a deeper understanding of investigative reporting processes and the confidence to undertake complex journalistic projects. Application Process and Deadline Applicants are required to submit a detailed application that demonstrates their interest in investigative journalism, their career goals, and how the fellowship will help them achieve those goals. Supporting materials may include writing samples, a resume, and references. Deadline: The application deadline varies annually. Applicants are strongly encouraged to check the official fellowship page and apply early to avoid missing out on this opportunity. Why You Should Apply The Susan Carroll Fellowship is more than just a training program; it is a career-defining opportunity for aspiring investigative journalists. It not only enhances technical skills but also provides a platform for fellows to connect with a global community of professionals who are dedicated to making a difference through journalism. For early-career journalists, this fellowship can serve as a stepping stone to greater opportunities, including positions in leading media organizations, collaborations on high-impact investigations, and recognition within the journalism community.Moreover, the fellowship’s focus on inclusivity ensures that journalists from diverse backgrounds have the opportunity to participate and contribute their unique perspectives to the field.In an era where credible information is more important than ever, initiatives like the Susan Carroll Fellowship play a vital role in strengthening the field of investigative journalism. By investing in the next generation of reporters, the fellowship helps ensure that important stories continue to be told and that accountability remains a cornerstone of society. If you are passionate about uncovering the truth and making a meaningful impact through journalism, the Susan Carroll Fellowship is an opportunity you should not overlook. Deadline: Varies annually (apply early) APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more fellowship opportunities, click here . title: Empowering Women for Climate Action: Sustainable Livelihoods and Community Resilience.
summary: Across the globe, the intersection of climate change and gender inequality continues to shape the lives of millions. Addressing these challenges requires innovative, inclusive, and community-driven solutions. At the forefront of this movement is SCWEI, an organization committed to empowering women while advancing sustainable climate action. About the Initiative SCWEI is dedicated to promoting sustainable climate solutions while uplifting underprivileged women. By integrating environmental action with economic empowerment, the organization is creating pathways for women to lead, thrive, and contribute meaningfully to their communities. Mission “To empower underprivileged women as leaders in climate resilience and sustainability, equipping them with essential skills and resources for dignified livelihoods and economic security. Through our initiatives, we bridge environmental action with economic opportunity, creating communities that thrive in harmony with nature and equality.” Vision “A sustainable world led by empowered women, where communities protect the environment and achieve lasting economic security, creating a future of equal opportunity, ecological balance, and shared prosperity.” Launch of the Community-Based Female Tailoring Training Center As part of its commitment to women’s empowerment, SCWEI has launched a Community-Based Female Tailoring Training Center. This initiative is designed to equip women with practical, income-generating skills that foster independence and long-term economic stability. The center recently hosted its first training session, attracting a strong and enthusiastic group of participants. The session was marked by a spirit of collaboration, determination, and hope, as women began their journey toward self-reliance and financial empowerment. This milestone reflects SCWEI’s broader goal of creating inclusive opportunities where women can transform their skills into sustainable livelihoods. By supporting women at the grassroots level, the organization is strengthening community resilience and promoting economic inclusion. Driving Sustainable Climate and Women Empowerment SCWEI’s work goes beyond skills training. The organization is deeply committed to advancing sustainable climate practices while empowering women through: Education and Capacity Building: Providing women with knowledge and tools to adapt to climate challenges. Advocacy: Promoting gender equality and environmental sustainability at local and global levels. Community Initiatives: Implementing projects that enhance climate resilience and improve livelihoods. These efforts contribute to building communities that are both environmentally sustainable and socially equitable. Join the Movement Climate change and gender inequality are interconnected challenges that require collective action. SCWEI invites individuals, partners, and communities to join hands in addressing these pressing issues. Support initiatives that empower women through skills and education Advocate for climate resilience and sustainable practices Participate in programs that drive community transformation Together, we can create a future where women lead climate action, communities thrive sustainably, and opportunities are accessible to all. Click Here to get involved. Empowering women is not just a social priority—it is a critical strategy for achieving sustainable development and climate resilience. Through initiatives like the Community-Based Female Tailoring Training Center, SCWEI is demonstrating how targeted interventions can transform lives and strengthen communities. By bridging the gap between environmental sustainability and economic opportunity, the organization is paving the way for a more inclusive, balanced, and prosperous world. title: Win $1 Million: Apply for the Yass Prize 2026 for Education Innovators in the U.S.
summary: Are you building a solution that is transforming education in the United States? The Yass Prize 2026 is now open, offering one of the most prestigious and competitive funding opportunities for education innovators. With a $1 million grand prize and access to a high-impact accelerator, this program is designed to support organizations that are reshaping how education is delivered and experienced. If you are an edtech founder, school leader, or nonprofit innovator, this is your chance to scale your impact and gain national recognition. Applications must be submitted by 1 June 2026 . About the Opportunity The Yass Prize is widely recognized as one of the leading awards for education innovation in the United States. Often referred to as the “Pulitzer for Education Innovation,” the program aims to identify and support organizations that are creating meaningful change in education systems. The initiative is built around the concept of STOP education —solutions that are Sustainable, Transformational, Outstanding, and Permissionless. These principles guide the selection of organizations that demonstrate strong potential to improve education outcomes while expanding access and opportunity. At the heart of the program is the Yass Prize Accelerator , a structured and immersive experience designed to help selected organizations refine their ideas, strengthen their strategies, and prepare for growth. Participants in the accelerator engage in a bootcamp that includes mentorship, training sessions, and exposure to leaders across education, policy, and business sectors. Throughout the program, participants will work on improving their business models, enhancing their storytelling, and preparing to pitch their solutions to a wider audience. The accelerator culminates in a competitive process where participants present their work and compete for the grand prize funding. Why This Opportunity Matters Education systems worldwide are undergoing rapid change, and innovation is essential to meet the needs of diverse learners. The Yass Prize plays a critical role in supporting organizations that are pushing boundaries and rethinking traditional approaches to education. By providing both funding and strategic support, the program enables innovators to test, refine, and scale their ideas , ultimately reaching more students and communities. It also fosters collaboration among leaders across sectors, creating a shared ecosystem of innovation and impact. This opportunity is especially important because it recognizes and supports solutions that not only improve academic outcomes but also promote dignity, inclusion, and long-term success for learners . What You’ll Gain Participating in the Yass Prize program offers significant benefits: Major Funding Opportunity: Compete for the $1 million grand prize, with additional funding opportunities available. Accelerator Experience: Join an intensive bootcamp focused on strategy, growth, and impact. Expert Guidance: Receive personalized feedback from industry experts and communication specialists. High-Level Exposure: Present your work to leaders across education, policy, and business sectors. Long-Term Network: Become part of a lifelong community of education innovators and changemakers. This opportunity is particularly valuable for organizations seeking to scale their solutions and attract investment or partnerships . Eligibility The Yass Prize is open to organizations and founders working in education within the United States. Eligible applicants include: EdTech companies Schools and microschools Nonprofit organizations Education-focused startups and initiatives Applicants should be developing innovative, scalable solutions that improve education outcomes and expand access to quality learning. The program is specifically focused on organizations operating within the United States , and applicants should demonstrate a strong commitment to transforming education systems. Application Process and Deadline To apply for the Yass Prize 2026: Review Eligibility: Ensure your organization meets the program criteria. Complete the Application: Submit your application through the official platform. Prepare for the Accelerator: Shortlisted applicants will be invited to participate in the Yass Prize Accelerator. Application Deadline: 1 June 2026 (12:00 PM EST) To learn more and apply, visit the official Yass Prize website. title: Apply Now: CBAI Summer AI Safety Fellowship 2026 in the USA (Fully Funded)
summary: The Cambridge Boston Alignment Initiative (CBAI) Summer Research Fellowship in AI Safety 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded program designed for aspiring researchers who want to build impactful careers in artificial intelligence safety. Taking place in Cambridge, Massachusetts , this intensive nine-week fellowship runs from June 8 to August 10, 2026 , offering participants the opportunity to work at the forefront of AI safety research. About the Fellowship The program brings together talented individuals to explore critical areas in AI safety, including: Interpretability of AI systems Multi-agent safety Formal verification Risk management frameworks AI governance and policy Fellows collaborate closely with experienced mentors and dedicated in-house research managers, gaining hands-on experience in tackling real-world AI safety challenges. In addition to research work, participants engage in weekly workshops, speaker sessions, and community events , connecting with experts and peers across top institutions such as Harvard, MIT, Northeastern University, and leading AI safety organizations. Fellowship Impact The inaugural cohort has already demonstrated impressive outcomes, including: Securing roles at leading AI safety organizations like Goodfire and Redwood Publishing research accepted at top conferences such as NeurIPS and ICLR Establishing independent research groups Sharing policy-relevant insights with decision-makers in Washington, D.C. This highlights the fellowship’s strong track record in launching successful AI safety careers. What the Fellowship Offers Participants will receive a comprehensive support package, including: 1. Generous Stipend $10,000 provided over the nine-week program 2. Accommodation Support Housing arranged for fellows coming from outside the Boston Metropolitan Area (via Harvard dorms or Airbnb) 3. Dedicated Workspace 24/7 access to office space in Harvard Square, just minutes from Harvard Yard 4. Mentorship Research Guidance High-touch support from in-house research managers Guidance to refine research direction and strengthen technical skills 5. Professional Development Structured opportunities to enhance research capabilities and career growth 6. Networking Opportunities Access to a vibrant AI safety community across top universities and organizations 7. Research Support Resources and collaboration opportunities to produce impactful work 8. Extension Fellowship Opportunities Potential pathways to continue research beyond the program Why You Should Apply If you’re passionate about shaping the future of artificial intelligence and ensuring its safe development, this fellowship offers a rare chance to: Gain hands-on research experience in AI safety Work alongside leading experts in the field Build a strong professional network Accelerate your career in a rapidly growing and impactful domain Application Deadline Deadline: April 12, 2026 APPLY HERE Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early to secure a spot in this highly competitive program. This fellowship is ideal for students, recent graduates, and early-career researchers who are committed to addressing some of the most pressing challenges in AI development and governance. title: Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon 2026: Build, Innovate, and Compete for $30,000+ in Prizes
summary: The rapid evolution of artificial intelligence has created unprecedented opportunities for developers, innovators, and creators worldwide. One of the most exciting platforms driving this innovation is Hack-Nation’s Global AI Hackathon 2026, a high-impact international event designed to bring together some of the brightest minds in technology to build real-world AI solutions in just 24 hours. Hack-Nation is an initiative rooted in the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ecosystem, with a mission centered on fostering innovation, collaboration, and rapid product development. As a nonprofit organization, it connects global talent from students to experienced developers and empowers them to turn ideas into functional prototypes under real startup-like pressure. What is the Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon? The Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon is an intensive, fast-paced innovation competition where participants collaborate to design, develop, and launch AI-powered solutions within a 24-hour sprint. Unlike traditional competitions, this hackathon emphasizes execution, creativity, and real-world application over theoretical concepts.Participants will work individually or in teams to solve open-ended challenges using artificial intelligence, machine learning, automation tools, and modern web technologies. The event is structured to simulate real startup environments, where speed, innovation, and adaptability are critical.The 2026 edition continues to expand its global footprint, attracting participants from over 60 countries and connecting them through both physical hubs and virtual platforms. Why You Should Participate Participating in the Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon offers far more than just competition. It is a gateway to global exposure, skill development, and career advancement. Access to Significant Prizes Participants compete for over $30,000 in prizes, including cash rewards and OpenAI API credits. Additionally, more than $150,000 worth of API credits will be available during the hackathon, enabling participants to build powerful and scalable AI solutions. Real-World Experience This hackathon is designed to mimic startup conditions. Participants experience the pressure of building and shipping products within tight deadlines, preparing them for real-world tech and entrepreneurial environments. Mentorship and Guidance Throughout the event, participants receive guidance from experienced mentors, including professionals from leading organizations such as Google and OpenAI. This mentorship enhances both technical and strategic thinking. Global Networking Opportunities Hack-Nation connects a diverse community of developers, designers, and innovators. Participants get the opportunity to collaborate with talented individuals from around the world, forming meaningful professional relationships. Career and Startup Opportunities Top-performing teams may gain access to further opportunities such as venture incubation programs, investor exposure, and startup support initiatives. Previous participants have gone on to join accelerator programs and build successful AI ventures. Event Timeline Hackathon Dates: April 25 – April 26, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early, as selection is competitive and spots are limited. Who Can Participate? The Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon is open to individuals who meet the legal age requirement in their country of residence. It welcomes participants from nearly all countries and territories, making it a truly global event.Whether you are a beginner exploring AI or an experienced developer building advanced systems, the hackathon provides an inclusive platform for all skill levels. What You Will Build Participants will focus on creating innovative AI-driven solutions that address real-world challenges. These may include AI-powered automation tools, healthcare and data-driven solutions, intelligent agents and assistants, business and productivity applications, and creative AI platforms.The hackathon encourages open-ended problem-solving, giving participants the freedom to explore ideas that have real impact. How to Apply To participate, applicants must complete the online registration form through the official platform. Due to the competitive nature of the hackathon, applicants are advised to present strong technical skills, creativity, and a clear interest in AI-driven innovation. The Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon 2026 is more than just a competition;it is an opportunity to challenge yourself, collaborate with global talent, and build solutions that could shape the future.With substantial prizes, world-class mentorship, and access to a global innovation network, this hackathon stands out as one of the most impactful AI events of the year. Application Deadline: April 11, 2026 APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, click here . For more opportunities on competitions , click here . title: Apply Now: African Union Call for Submissions (Up to $50,000 Grants) for EdTech, TVET, AI and Skills Development
summary: The Department of Education, Science, Technology, and Innovation of the African Union has officially launched the Innovating Education in Africa (IEA) 2026 Call for Submissions , inviting innovators across the continent to propose transformative solutions that address Africa’s most pressing education and skills development challenges. Background: Why Innovation in Education Matters Despite notable progress, education systems across Africa continue to face persistent challenges in access, equity, quality, relevance, and skills alignment . Key statistics highlight the urgency: 20% of children aged 6–11 are out of school 33% of children aged 12–14 are excluded Nearly 60% of youth aged 15–17 remain out of education These challenges disproportionately affect girls and are compounded by: Shortage and uneven distribution of qualified teachers Limited professional development opportunities Weak alignment between education outcomes and labor market needs At higher levels, systems such as Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) and universities face: Outdated curricula and weak industry linkages Limited digital integration and AI adoption Insufficient research capacity and innovation ecosystems To address these issues, Africa needs scalable, cost-effective, and system-integrated innovations that strengthen education systems and align with continental strategies. About Innovating Education in Africa (IEA) Launched in 2018 by the African Union, IEA aims to identify, promote, and scale impactful education innovations across Africa. Impact so far: 1,500+ education stakeholders engaged 180 innovations supported Up to USD 1 million mobilized In 2026, the initiative expands its scope to include: Basic and secondary education Higher education and research ecosystems TVET and skills development Digital systems and AI integration Green skills and sustainability 2026 Thematic Focus Areas IEA 2026 is seeking innovations in the following key areas: 1. Basic and Secondary Education Foundational literacy and numeracy technologies Teacher professional development solutions AI-enabled assessment and adaptive learning Inclusive and gender-responsive education tools 2. Higher Education STI Ecosystems University-industry innovation hubs Research commercialization platforms Digital universities and hybrid learning systems Robotics and STEM centers of excellence Science parks, labs, and incubators 3. TVET and Skills Development AI-integrated training models Robotics and automation training Green and climate-responsive skills Apprenticeship and dual training systems Skills certification aligned with AfCFTA 4. AI, Robotics, and Emerging Technologies Robotics labs in schools AI-powered skills forecasting tools Simulation-based technical training Youth innovation hubs AI ethics, governance, and data privacy solutions Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be citizens of an African Union Member State Lead a legally registered organization Be implementing an education innovation with verifiable results Be directly responsible for the innovation (not intermediaries) Demonstrate scalability and alignment with continental frameworks Note: Past IEA grant recipients are not eligible. Application Process Applications must be submitted online in English or French and include: Problem statement (100 words) Innovation description (500 words) Performance and impact report (500 words) Contribution to digital, green, AI, or skills ecosystems Proof of business registration Apply here Deadline: 30 April 2026, 23:00 EAT (GMT+3) Selection Process and Timeline The evaluation process includes: Eligibility Screening: 1–8 May 2026 Technical Review: 8–20 May 2026 Early Pitch (Virtual): May 2026 Final Pitch: June 2026 Final outcome: 50 shortlisted innovations 10 early pitch finalists 5 final pitch candidates 3 winners awarded grants Evaluation Criteria Submissions will be assessed based on: Innovation and uniqueness Scalability and sustainability Measurable impact Integration with public systems Industry relevance (for TVET) Gender and climate responsiveness Alignment with AU strategies (CESA, CTVET, STISA) Potential for commercialization and technology transfer Benefits for Selected Innovators Successful applicants will receive: Grants of up to USD 50,000 Recognition from the African Union Promotion across AU Member States and partners Publication in the Africa Education Skills Innovations Handbook 2026 Capacity-building support Participation in the IEA 2026 Expo and policy dialogues Opportunities to influence national education policies Inclusion in continental AI readiness initiatives Explore more details about the grant here The Innovating Education in Africa 2026 Call is more than a funding opportunity—it is a platform to shape the future of education across the continent . By supporting bold, scalable, and impactful solutions, the African Union aims to build inclusive, resilient, and future-ready education systems aligned with Africa’s long-term development goals. Discover many other grant opportunities Here. title: Apply Now: Join the US Refugee Advisory Board (USRAB) 2026 and Shape Global Refugee Policy
summary: Are you a leader with lived experience of forced displacement and a passion for influencing policy? The United States Refugee Advisory Board (USRAB) is now accepting applications for its third cohort of Advisors. This unique opportunity allows individuals to directly contribute to global and national policy discussions, ensuring that refugee voices are represented where decisions are made. Applications must be submitted by 31 March 2026 . About the Opportunity The United States Refugee Advisory Board (USRAB) is a self-governing initiative designed to ensure that forcibly displaced individuals have a meaningful and equitable role in policymaking processes . The board is coordinated by Refugee Congress , in partnership with Refugee Council USA (RCUSA) and the Institute for the Study of International Migration (ISIM) at Georgetown University . USRAB engages with a wide range of stakeholders, including the United States Government , the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) , civil society organizations, and refugee-led groups. Its mission is to ensure that the perspectives, knowledge, and lived experiences of displaced people are integrated into decision-making processes that affect their lives and communities. Selected Advisors will serve a three-year term , contributing to strategic discussions, policy engagement, and community consultations. The role is designed to create a transparent and accountable mechanism through which refugee voices can shape policies at both national and international levels. Why This Opportunity Matters Refugees and forcibly displaced individuals are often underrepresented in decision-making processes that directly impact their lives. The USRAB initiative addresses this gap by ensuring that those with lived experience are not only included but are central to shaping policies and programs. By creating a platform for meaningful participation, USRAB promotes equity, inclusion, and accountability in global migration and refugee governance. Advisors play a critical role in bridging the gap between policymakers and affected communities, ensuring that policies are informed by real experiences and practical insights. This opportunity is particularly impactful because it goes beyond consultation—it enables individuals to actively influence policy outcomes , contribute to strategic discussions, and engage in high-level dialogues with global stakeholders. What You’ll Gain Serving as a USRAB Advisor provides several unique benefits: Policy Influence: Directly contribute to shaping refugee and migration policies at national and global levels. Leadership Development: Strengthen your skills in advocacy, governance, and strategic engagement. Global Exposure: Engage with international organizations, policymakers, and civil society leaders. Networking Opportunities: Connect with a diverse community of advocates, experts, and stakeholders. Capacity Building: Participate in training and collaborative initiatives to enhance your impact. Although this is a volunteer position , all approved travel and participation costs related to USRAB activities are covered, ensuring accessibility for selected Advisors. Eligibility USRAB is seeking applicants who meet the following criteria: Individuals with lived experience of forced displacement are strongly encouraged to apply Demonstrated professional expertise or engagement in relevant fields such as advocacy, policy, or community work Ability to actively participate in meetings, consultations, and project-based work Willingness and ability to travel within the United States and internationally for meetings and events Availability to commit approximately 5–15 hours per month Applicants must also be able to participate in required training and engagements, as well as contribute meaningfully to the board’s work. Application Process and Deadline To apply for the USRAB Advisor cohort: Complete the Application Form: Fill out the official application form carefully. Prepare Required Documents: Submit your resume and two professional references . Submit Before Deadline: Ensure your application is complete and submitted on time. Incomplete applications will not be considered. Applications take approximately 30 minutes to complete. The original deadline has been extended to April 24th, 2026. To learn more, visit the official Refugee Congress website . You can find the form to apply here. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Women in Energy Fellowship Program 2026: Applications now open
summary: The Women in Energy Fellowship is a prestigious career development opportunity designed to empower women professionals in the global energy sector. This highly competitive fellowship provides a transformative platform for early-career women to accelerate their professional growth, gain cutting-edge industry knowledge, and build powerful international networks. If you are a woman seeking to grow your career in energy, climate, sustainability, or related industries, this fellowship represents a rare opportunity to gain global exposure and leadership skills that can shape your future. Overview of the Women in Energy Fellowship The Women in Energy Fellowship is an annual, in-person professional development program hosted by leading global institutions such as Columbia University’s Center on Global Energy Policy. It brings together talented women from across the world who are actively working in the energy sector and are committed to advancing into leadership roles. The fellowship is specifically structured to: Enhance participants’ technical and leadership skills Provide exposure to the latest advancements in the global energy landscape Build a strong professional network of women leaders in energy Support long-term career progression into senior leadership roles Participants engage in workshops, discussions, and networking sessions with top experts, policymakers, and industry leaders. Who Can Apply: Eligible Candidates This fellowship targets a specific category of professionals to ensure high impact and relevance. Primary Target Group Women working in the energy sector Early to mid-career professionals Experience Requirement Typically 3 to 8 years of professional experience in energy or related fields Ideal Candidates Applicants should: Be committed to a long-term career in energy Demonstrate leadership potential Show a clear desire to influence the future of the energy sector Eligible Countries and Regions The Women in Energy Fellowship is generally open to international applicants, meaning: Applicants from Africa, Europe, Asia, North America, South America, and Oceania can apply There is no strict country limitation in most cases However, selected fellows must be able to attend in-person sessions (e.g., in New York, USA) independently This makes it a truly global opportunity, including strong eligibility for candidates from: Kenya Nigeria South Africa India United Kingdom United States And many more Key Benefits of the Women in Energy Fellowship This fellowship is not just a training program—it is a career accelerator. Selected fellows gain access to: 1. Advanced Industry Knowledge Participants learn about: Energy transition and sustainability Climate policy and global energy systems Emerging technologies in clean energy 2. Leadership Development The program equips fellows with: Strategic thinking skills Leadership training Communication and negotiation techniques 3. Global Networking Opportunities Fellows connect with: Industry leaders Policymakers Fellow professionals from across the globe This network often becomes a long-term career asset. 4. Career Advancement Support The fellowship helps participants: Position themselves for leadership roles Expand career opportunities internationally Gain recognition in the energy sector Program Structure and Format The Women in Energy Fellowship is typically: In-person (hosted in New York or similar global hubs) Conducted over a defined program period Composed of: Workshops Panel discussions Expert lectures Networking events Participants are expected to actively engage and contribute throughout the program. Why This Fellowship Matters The global energy sector is undergoing a massive transformation, driven by: Climate change Renewable energy adoption Policy shifts Despite this, women remain underrepresented in leadership roles. The Women in Energy Fellowship directly addresses this gap by: Empowering women professionals Promoting gender equality in energy leadership Building a pipeline of future female leaders The program envisions a world where women are equally represented at all levels of the energy sector. Application Requirements To apply successfully, candidates should prepare: Required Documents Updated CV/Resume Statement of Interest outlining: Career goals Motivation for applying Interest in the energy sector Additional supporting documents (if required) What Selection Committees Look For Strong academic or professional background Demonstrated passion for energy and sustainability Leadership experience or potential Clear vision for career growth Step-by-Step Application Process Visit the official application page (via the provided link) Complete the online application form Upload all required documents Submit before the deadline Wait for shortlisting and final selection notification Shortlisted candidates may be contacted for further evaluation. Apply here Deadline 26th April 2026 The Women in Energy Fellowship stands out as one of the most impactful global opportunities for women in the energy field. It combines education, mentorship, and networking into a powerful experience that can redefine your career trajectory. For women in Africa and beyond, this fellowship is more than just an opportunity—it is a gateway into global leadership in one of the most important sectors shaping the future of our world.
